reviews.text: I have tried iPhone 14 and 16 and the app kicks me out once I try pushing a problem. Worked fine until a recent update.
reviews.title: Not working on Apple phones

reviews.text: App crashes when selecting report category, rendering the app useless. Refund cities until this is fixed. You are stealing taxpayer money.
reviews.title: Show stopper crash

reviews.text:
App consistently crashed when selecting “Park Issue”, so I had to change to “Sidewalk Issue” to get my issue submitted.

Spent 10 minutes, looking for a way to log the bug, but no success. You apparently need some kind of special login to log a bug.
reviews.title: Unable to report bug

reviews.text: Multiple requests have been sent to have severe street damage fixed with no response. Another request was sent multiple times for a vehicle that hasn’t moved since at least last July with no response
reviews.title: Unresponsive

reviews.text: I love the fact that the County can be notified of an issue that needs to addressed.  One can submit photos of the issue to assist in further identification.  The County overseer can then send the problem to the correct County Department.  Job well done.
reviews.title: Notification of County Issues

reviews.text: This is a must for any county - it gives citizens the opportunity to report on things they see that need fixing or addressing. You may see a fallen sign or trash on the roadway. Now you can report it!
reviews.title: Great Tool!

reviews.text:
SeeClickFix turned out to be a successful program.  Almondridge has seen dramatic positive change around the neighborhood.  
Thank you
reviews.title: Thank you

reviews.text: There is no way to close an issue within the app.
reviews.title: Missing features

reviews.text: Clark County Nevada is 100% useless in responding to SeeClickFix reports. I watch multiple reports being created for the same incident over months and nothing gets done. Thanks Clark County.
reviews.title: Not really SeeClickFix’s fault

reviews.text:
All this app does is makes me see how bad my city is with all the derelict crime, homeless issues, unwillingness to fix or make info structure better.  My city takes forever to acknowledge fixes or even fix them at all. Many times they just bi—tch about how their city workers are hard workers when in reality it is the opposite. Pd are corrupt in renton and enforce nothing. People speed galore in my town since pd stopped enforcing speed limit. 

Citizens keep pushing to defund pd and having resided in towns that did that: let me warn you that you run great risk by doing that. Gangs will over run the city and you have to know personal numbers of minute men that offer protection. You don’t want lawlessness in a society where half the people need law or it will crumble. Stop trying to push defund pd. If you want to experience this your self live in one of those towns. You will move out fast.

What’s the point i put fixes in and mg town ignores them. Speeding is out of control and people seem to do what ever the hell they want with little to no recourse.
reviews.title: Settings are a bit off for gps

reviews.text: I made an account and my city that I live in does use this and resolves issues quickly. However, I get several emails everyday of other posts in my neighborhood, have unsubscribed to everything, and messaged support THREE times now asking them to delete my account (it always tells me there is an error when I try to do it myself…). So, I would highly suggest just not using this platform unless you enjoy email spam and no support from their support email.
reviews.title: No support with app

reviews.text: I would like to see you add an other description add to the list. I needed to put in an issue about snow removal but there wasn’t a specific one on the list.
reviews.title: Snow removal

reviews.text: I be found the app to be quite useful in reporting issues such as graffiti, potholes, and other public safety issues. Seems to have some ongoing odd GPS location issues where it defaults to a specific address. Also I've noticed that there are a handful of posters responsible for a disproportionate number of reported issues including some very fickle issues such as a single candy wrapper next to a public walkway. I think the app could benefit from some moderating in such instances.
reviews.title: Good app no some issues,

reviews.text: Need more communication to the community
reviews.title: Communication

reviews.text: The city been doing a great job as soon as I put a request, they go clean everything fast I like it. Keep the streets clean great job.
reviews.title: Illegal dumping

reviews.text: Thanks to responders!
reviews.title: Thanks

reviews.text: Thank you Public Works for taking care of this issue in Northridge Subdivision’
reviews.title: Vacant overgrown lots

reviews.text: Excellent that you describe the bugs you fixed.
reviews.title: Details

reviews.text:
The City ignores most complaints.
Example: the terrible open sewer smell in North Shore Park, particularly behind the pool, is caused by a buildup of Sargassum seaweed. This should be collected and removed. Multiple complaints have been made by multiple people over the past few weeks and nothing gets done
reviews.title: Real Issues ignored by City

reviews.text: Garbage
reviews.title: Text input on mobile is broken

reviews.text: Nice idea but super buggy.  The app scrambles text I enter in the description and then select a dropdown.  Also when I comment on an issue it sometimes doesn’t appear, but when I close the app,reopen the app, and recomment thinking that it failed to submit- it now has both comments.
reviews.title: Nice idea but very buggy

reviews.text: Reported multiple abandoned vehicles and nothing was ever done
reviews.title: Actions

reviews.text: Was just told by a city employee that this app is no longer in service. New app is NBConnected.
reviews.title: New app

reviews.text: The app is extremely useful and effective when it works, but it has a lot of bugs with iOS 26. Entered text gets garbled when making selections from the drop downs. When it works, it works and if the reports are detailed, honest, and well intentioned, repairs often occur, which is great for the city and its residents. If they update the app for iOS 26, it will be much more useful, but they have not done that yet.
reviews.title: Good, when it works, but buggy in iOS 26

reviews.text: The app is garbling text in the description field for some users.
reviews.title: Great when it works

reviews.text: Filter closed/acknowledged cases and more than half the map will reflect neglected
reviews.title: This app has 0 cases closed (all time) for Tampa and cities near by

reviews.text: 8/28/25 - I installed this to use in Chicago but, then, saw the requests were from over a decade ago. Looks like no one uses this in chicago probably because there’s CHI311. Too bad, this would have been nice bc this app allows viewing the photos that were submitted with service requests.
reviews.title: Not for Chicago

reviews.text: To find requests without hours of spare time for scrolling I kindly request a filter based on type of request. Real life example: Dozens of illegal dumping requests that I don’t want to see are effectively hiding the few entries I do want to see unless i pick through one by one. Luckily computing devices are great at this very thing!
reviews.title: Better filters please

reviews.text:
See click fix, has been very useful to report things such as pot holes, unlicensed auto sales for. Homes and sidewalks that need clear g.
I suggest we have a field that allow us to report vehicles registers out of state but always on mlocla property. This type of w braking causes oss of $1.3M in Stamford and that is not catching all
Of them.
Aunt some similar way we can report specifically for out of state vehicle, that don't pau local insurance, race, etc.
reviews.title: Useful resource most of the time- w/ suggestion License Plates tax evaders out of state.

reviews.text: This app works very well for my purposes to communicate property needs that should be reported .  Action is almost always taken and notifications are sent.
reviews.title: Easy effective system!

reviews.text: The app makes it super easy to file incidents around the city of Bellaire and Houston without waiting needlessly for an operator.
reviews.title: SeeClickFix /311 Easy to Report

reviews.text: Excellent turnaround time! Thank you!
reviews.title: Repairs to potholes on Jefferson avenue in front of Ste Anne’s  & over the bridge

reviews.text: Can we please get an easy to complete form? every time someone dumps garbage I have to click the same 20 stupid buttons.
reviews.title: Stupid 20 click forms

reviews.text: Love the idea, but I can’t do anything with this if the login doesn’t work. I’ve reset my password a few times now on a browser, but when I go to log in on the app I get an error. That means I can’t contribute or do anything at all.
reviews.title: Cool but there’s issues

reviews.text: I’m really grateful for how quickly my concern is recognized
reviews.title: Grateful

reviews.text: Good app, but when the app tells me there may be duplicates, the list doesn't show enough detail to determine whether the listed items are indeed duplicate. Please include a few lines of text for each possible duplicate.
reviews.title: Show more text for duplicates

reviews.text:
Some agencies click “resolved” to indicate they have dispensed with the matter. It’s out of their hands, as if they fixed the issue. 

But sometimes they’ve only referred it. It is out of their hands; but to the person notifying via Click… it’s not resolved at all. 

Perhaps there could be more options for the agency. “Referred” — then STATE WHAT AGENCY so the customer can follow up if accountability is needed. Also, perhaps another something to justify any delay, like waiting for parts, waiting for the mission to return fr Mars, etc. 

Thank you. I really should give this five stars.
reviews.title: “Resolved” needs to be changed

reviews.text: The app on iOS has a keyboard bug that causes gliding while typing to insert extra random and jumbled characters. Please fix this. Thank you.
reviews.title: Keyboard bug

reviews.text: I think it’s a really good site. They told us about this at the city that we saw something that was graffiti to report it. They were gonna clean up. We really appreciate this. Thank you.
reviews.title: Thank uou

reviews.text: This app is a waste and just another knee jerk way the Mayor of Paterson is trying to suppress the public . The site is not user friendly and isn’t monitored in real time .
reviews.title: Horrible App

reviews.text: Our hill is always forgotten about when it comes to sanding unless someone complains. The sander always goes up to the end of Russ St and never continues down Door to Vinton. Please be sure to take care of our hill. Thank you
reviews.title: Vinton St/McKim

reviews.text: Garbage app that can’t find my location even though location services are on. Putting in a pin on the map lets me proceed but then a search of the categories returns a blank result so impossible to report anything.
reviews.title: Useless

reviews.text: Great way to report an issue needing work
reviews.title: Quick Response

reviews.text: The app for whatever reason removes all punctuation from a ticket description. This has been a problem for years. Why hasn’t this simple bug been fixed?
reviews.title: Removes all punctuation in description

reviews.text: É lente
reviews.title: Good

reviews.text: I don’t know what Public Works repaired on Wednesday evening but YOU GUYS ROCK!  We have lived in Skyline for 8 years. The next day, I had the best shower of my life in 8 years!! On Saturday I could run my dishwasher and washing machine at the same time and it didn’t take forever for both of them to fill.  Whatever you repaired made a huge difference!!!  Thank you for all your hard work.  Pam Balentine
reviews.title: Water

reviews.text: From the first time I uploaded a photo no what what I select to upload only the very first photo I ever used will appear in any report
reviews.title: Useful but buggy

reviews.text:
Sometimes—like to report an abandoned car in your neighborhood—this app requires multiple tiny details, many of which I can only guess at

But usually it’s a quick way to alert the city to non-emergency stuff that needs attention: a new pothole dangerous to cyclists, street lights out, suspicious water flows (busted sewer?) etc
reviews.title: Mostly fine way to alert city officials

reviews.text: Left untouched
reviews.title: Left unfinished

reviews.text: A quick way to report issues.  Low bar for the amount of hassle with high results from the city.   Love it
reviews.title: Easy peasy

reviews.text: Thank you very much for the job well done with this. These have been a problem for some time and the coverage your guys did should help immensely.
reviews.title: Potholes

reviews.text: Within days of reporting a damaged storm water drain with rebar exposed, the city installed a brand new one making that spot in our neighborhood where many people walk daily safe again. See Click Fix is not only a time saver, but since it is so easy to report issues, I am happy to keep submitting instead of trying to make phone calls and send emails. Wonderful tool!
reviews.title: Hazard fixed quickly!

reviews.text: The clicked fixed are closed before the worked is even started!
reviews.title: Click Fix Closed

reviews.text:
This app, unfortunately, is down to having only a single developer working on it and he’s limited to just 30min per week. 

If you try to use punctuation when opening a ticket, the app will remove it when you hit post. As a result, you’re going to look like an angry and illiterate lunatic when you submit your request. 

This has been a bug for years. The latest iteration of the bug removes all punctuation from your ticket and then just appends it to the end as an unbroken series of periods, commas, and question marks.
reviews.title: Years later, punctuation is still broken

reviews.text: I do not know if the app works or not, I do know that the city pays  no attention to it or simply does not care, because they do not enforce the ordinance
reviews.title: Cars parked in yard

reviews.text: App loses locations and filters resets while working. Not good.
reviews.title: Not consistent

reviews.text: I’ve used this app before to good effect but lately requests just vanish down a hole. No acknowledgement that the request was received and certainly no action taken.
reviews.title: No response

reviews.text: Thanks for responding so quickly- it’s a great feeling to get rid of broken things!!!
reviews.title: Big Thank You!!!

reviews.text: See click fix seems to be a useful tool for bringing non critical issues to the attention of city workers. In the hands of self-appointed watchdogs with beefs against their neighbors it is sometimes abused. Some of the issues seem like they would be better resolved by knocking on your neighbor’s door and discussing the matter. I wish it was possible to comment on individual complaints.
reviews.title: What’s the beef?

reviews.text: I am very pleased with this app, they keep on top of things. Thank you, all for your hard work.
reviews.title: On top of it

reviews.text: This app rarely opens, always says: Oops, try again.
reviews.title: Not great

reviews.text: I sent a request last week to see, click, fix about the fire hazard danger of overgrown weeds/bushes on the fire road behind the houses on Sunmeadow St.  The crew literally had it cleared early the week after my request.  Thank you crew! Job well done!
reviews.title: Fire road hazard clearance -Sunmeadow St

reviews.text: You guys are the best. Thanx
reviews.title: Thanx

reviews.text: Tried and tried but couldn’t post an image with my request.  Wish my locality would pick a new usable service.
reviews.title: Cannot post with an image from my iPhone

reviews.text:
No way to delete or edit an entry.

Please add the option to delete my own entry.
reviews.title: No delete option

reviews.text: This was handled by the trash collection team ages ago. I called them directly and they were apologetic, professional, respectful and efficient. They picked it up before noon the same day. See Click Fix is only responding NOW. My post was months ago. lol so it’s closed.
reviews.title: Trash Uncollected

reviews.text: Living on this alley has made me realize that much of the damage is also due to the amount of traffic using it as a through street. Many patrons leaving the pharmacy head north instead of exiting to 9th. The alley is busier than Lincoln Street to the front of my house. My wife and I are both retired and see this daily out our backyard. A no left turn sign from the pharmacy might be in order.
reviews.title: Alley between 8th/9th

reviews.text: Commercial truckers expanding in residential neighborhood now a fleet with 55 feet long trailers.
reviews.title: Still waiting

reviews.text: Too many questions, hard to navigate, and potholes don't get fixed. See, 100 clicks, no fix.
reviews.title: False advertising

reviews.text:
I spoke with a clerk at the county of San Bernardino regarding an issue with the gravel along the road washing away, and was told to either email the issue with pictures or use the app.  The email address that I wrote down, came back undeliverable, so I was going to submit it on the app. The app indicates the address is not part of the county and would not let me proceed to submit the request.

The app needs to be corrected.  The address has been a part of the county for a very, very long time. It is not part of the city.  The address is located on Kendall between Yucca Street and Little League Drive and is part of the county responsibility.
reviews.title: Location of an issue indicates it is not part of the county area

reviews.text: Tracks the request and gives the city records of work requested and work completed.
reviews.title: Great information tool!

reviews.text: I like the ease of reporting and the overall functions of the app. However there should be a way for the government entities to not be able to close a report without verification by a third party or the person who submitted the issue. A county near me posts an automated answer with vague information and many times the issue is not actually handled.  Or they say they have handled it even though it’s an ongoing issue.
reviews.title: Automated messages

reviews.text: Crashes. Loses your content. Completely unintuitive.
reviews.title: This app is sooooo janky

reviews.text: I’ve been successful reporting things that need to be fixed.  But, once fixed I keep getting notified that the issue is still open.  If there is a way to close an issue, it’s not obvious.
reviews.title: Useful but hard to stop

reviews.text: I have used SCF to report illegal signs on the public right-of-way for years. However, there are times when Public Works fails to do their job and then replies on the complaint that it has been done. This is particularly annoying when they turn off “Public commenting” so that there is only one way to respond, ….. and that involves making a new request. This has happened recently at 3946 Northside Drive in Macon, GA.  Public Works has falsely stated (3 times) that the illegal signs and the illegal sign under construction had been removed. As of right now, the steel sign posts and the wooden sign posts are still in the same place, the public right-of-way. Someone in authority needs to step up and take responsibility.
reviews.title: Mixed Results

reviews.text: We don’t see results yet. We see over a dozen empty buildings that are weathered, ruined, and abandoned construction sites. Some with no roof for over a decade. All my reports have been closed with no resolution.
reviews.title: I see you’re kinda responding

reviews.text: Reported several abandoned vehicles. No response or activity seen for these issues. Vehicles are still there nearly 5 months after I reported. I don’t think this service is even monitored anymore.
reviews.title: No service

reviews.text: It is amazing the problems in the neighborhood that the CITY OF FOLSOM has been so proactive in taking care of certain situations in our neighborhoods. I. E. abandoned vehicles/ cars parked on the lawn / tall dried weeds that had the potential of a fire threatening homes.
reviews.title: Taking Care of Business

reviews.text: Utility of the app is significantly reduced because the only way to attach a photo is most recent or camera. One cannot go to their library, and one cannot select multiple photos to upload. This functionality used to work in earlier versions of the app, but seems to not work. When trying to upload those photos from the iPhone library, the app goes back to the starting screen with no results. They need to fix this for the app to have any utility
reviews.title: Photos do not work right

reviews.text:
Should be called SeeClickBouncePuzzleBouncePuzzleMaybeFix

Requires a street address even when I’ve geo-located the problem on my phone, so if the problem is along a park, as they often are, this app tries to put in an address on another nearby street. And the best I can correct this is to manually type in an address of a home adjacent to the park, on the right street but a block away. After having to look up that address in Google Maps. 

My municipality has found it necessary to implement about a dozen questions/checks for an issue, and even if I haven’t answered them all (because they are off the screen in the app), the app app still lets me hit Submit, then gives me a big error message in little tiny type. You could say that’s the fault of my city’s implementation, but it still makes this app very difficult and time-consuming to use. I end up standing by the side of the road for 15-20 minutes trying to get it all straight.
reviews.title: Very clumsy, frustrating, time-consuming

reviews.text: Not maintained
reviews.title: Sidewalks and bike routes !!!

reviews.text: Large pot hole.
reviews.title: Pot hole Cheatham av

reviews.text: I’ve reported everything from LP playground being muddy and needing mulch, to a 1 foot sinkhole on Larned at Rivertown Market. It all gets fixed! Wow! So grateful!
reviews.title: Very happy!

reviews.text: Amazed at how expeditiously the city responded to the pothole threatening my low profile WRX! That was awesome! They came earlier than me leaving, and filled it so I could leave safely and begin my day! Big up to that street crew!!! Don’t know how it will stand up to the plows this winter… but we’ll cross that bridge if we get to it. Meantime… great job street patch crew!!! Ya’ll rock!!!💯💪🏽
reviews.title: Expeditiously Done!

reviews.text: I am unable to make new actual requests, only post to neighbors. None of mine have been even acknowledged in months, when they used to get fixed within the week. Can’t find anywhere why. Their Q&A section only says that posting to neighbors is an option, but for me there are no other options.
reviews.title: Blocked me from making requests

reviews.text: Can’t edit or delete post.
reviews.title: Can’t edit or delete post.

reviews.text: I keep getting notices that this issue is still open and that I should close it out if it has been resolved. First, that seems backwards, but there is no mechanism on the app for me to do that.
reviews.title: Closing Issues

reviews.text:
I disabled photo access for the app.
I verified in my iPhone settings that I disabled access.

The app can still access my entire photo library.
reviews.title: App can access photos despite not allowing access

reviews.text: App doesn't allow users to edit requests. App incorrectly uploads wrong photos to requests. PLEASE FIX
reviews.title: Bug Fix and Edit Options

reviews.text:
Problem was fixed within 24 hours.
Thank you.
reviews.title: Thank you

reviews.text: Why when you state to close a tickets it doesn’t close and keeps popping up
reviews.title: Close

reviews.text: I've sent in the same request 3 times. With being told the route supervisor has the information. Don't know what y'all pay him but he's not done anything for me in 2 months. We have huge trash piles on Hancock place and Idlewood drive also. Haven't seen a truck in months. So this app doesn't work for me.
reviews.title: No Responce.

reviews.text:
Together.
This app gives me a WE feeling in my city. Like I can make it better.
And my city?
They are all over it ❤️
reviews.title: WE can do something

reviews.text: We couldn’t ever get along without them ! Thank you , Thank you !
reviews.title: Best City Service Auburn has ever offered !

reviews.text: I wish this app was easier to use.  I could report a lot more problems. I find I cannot get the address right. Sometimes when driving it’s impossible to get an address.
reviews.title: Good app but I find it difficult

reviews.text: To report and to get results is usually quite good! Where are you are sorely lacking and have been for years is when you report back to say that the problem has been fixed you don’t receive or ignore that so 1065 days later I still get the same question on the same issue over and over again that’s where this app really needs to be fixed! Somebody is dropping the ball at the end and it’s frustrating.  And I’d like to add that, it doesn’t matter how many times I report back thatThe issue has been fixed… Still get the question a few months later asking if it has it been fixed…. some of my issues have been close to 2000 days waiting for you guys to get the message. So fix that issue and you’ll get five stars for me! And thank you for the survey‼️
reviews.title: Feedback

reviews.text: This is an excellent way to request help!!
reviews.title: Cool

reviews.text: HSV City Landscape came quickly & did a super job clearing space for egress in our alley. Personable kind courteous! The best!
reviews.title: High weeds in alley

reviews.text: 95% of the time that I report stuff on this app it gets done and it gets done immediately. Great app!!!!
reviews.title: Great program

reviews.text: Maybe it’s just that I have an old apple phone, but this Ap needs to remember the phone owner. It’s ridiculous have to type in your name, address and phone number each time. Also, the checks to acknowledge the rules is almost invisible and they are not on the same side of the page as other boxes. Make those boxes bold!
reviews.title: Needs to be more user friendly

reviews.text: I want to thank See Click Fix and the staff in Lake Elsinore for their prompt and thorough work. Cleaning up other people junk is not a pleasant job, but our residents truly appreciate your efforts. You keep our community clean and presentable and ultimately improve our property value. Thank you for everything you do!
reviews.title: Thank you!

reviews.text: And they get right on the complaint, what ever the situation, how amazing 🤩 is that
reviews.title: Amazing 🎁

reviews.text: It was better before they improved it. There’s very little information and it’s hard to find. It seems rather static and of little use. even when things get done it’s hard to track when they got done. It’s sort of an after the fact reporting. All you can tell is that it got assigned to somebody but that was obvious before. it used to be much more interactive.
reviews.title: More confusion, less information

reviews.text: ClickFix allows me to ride my bike, photo graffiti, overgrown vegetation blocking the sidewalk, broken concrete, random hazards - whatever needs attention - public or private - and pass it on to the City of Hemet to add to their list of things to do.
reviews.title: Volunteer

reviews.text: Really wish this app had a way to edit or delete a post after submitting. At least twice I’ve accidentally swiped a finger (yes, my fault) and ended up with incomplete postings.  After hitting “submit” it would be very helpful if there was a message something like “Once submitted your post cannot be edited or deleted, are you ready to submit at this time?”
reviews.title: Can’t edit/delete post after submitting

reviews.text: I was happy the Malden tree trimmers came and took care in trimming the tree in front of my house. Nice job and clean up.
reviews.title: Great job

reviews.text: A big tree fell across the street and it took less than a half hour for them to show up with all the needed equipment to quickly and professionally clear the road. Friendly workers who cleaned up after themselves and had smiles the whole time. Thank you.
reviews.title: Incredibly great service. Really!

reviews.text: The code violation is too hard to find and use. most people give up before they find he space
reviews.title: Code violation.

reviews.text: We submit many tickets to the city road department and they are frequently not completed.
reviews.title: Repairs Not Made When Reported

reviews.text:
It seems like the only goal of this app is to push an issue onto someone else. I submitted that an ottoman has been dumped at a bus stop. It went from one department to another before I was told by King County Transportation that it’s been there a while, so weee just going to leave it. Now there’s more crap over there. 
I just submitted a request for a broken or leaking water main at the Crestview Park. Just got a notice that it’s been closed because I should contact the water department. 

Useless. Glad we’re spending money on an app that is not maintained and only exists for some government employee to have one more box to check everyday.
reviews.title: Worse interactions ever

reviews.text: I sent an email requesting information on why there is not a baseball/softball scoreboard.  NEVER RECEIVED A REPLY.
reviews.title: Score board at Memorial

reviews.text: On the app I have not been able to close an open ticket I started when the problem is resolved. As a result I end up getting multiple emails asking to close. Please take a look at the app for iPhones. KS
reviews.title: Problems closing posts

reviews.text:
The city of Marlborough MA uses this app. I’ve reported a couple of issues and they always respond within the same day. Not necessarily to fix the issue but to let me know it’s on their agenda. Certainly useful in closing the time between rude things happening and getting a fix. 

A great public service tool!
reviews.title: It’s a local and mobile thing

reviews.text:
I appreciate it, this tool fir community organizations and block captains are essential. We are able to get action on issues close to our hearts reference safety. 
Baba Bob Shipman, Philadephia Pa
reviews.title: Community Savior Tool

reviews.text: I discussed with Hanna this morning in Abilene
reviews.title: Nothing accomplished in this app

reviews.text: the burden on taxpayers to do the city's maintenance work, isn't the way city maintenance is supposed to happen. In return for tax dollars, the city should fix the problems such as illegal dumping and parking violations (which are obvious if the city took an hour to drive around the neighborhood) without making taxpayers take the time to report each issue piecemeal. Instead the city uses this app to pretend it's effectively listening to taxpayers when it doesn't even fix most of the problems reported. What a joke.
reviews.title: App doesn't let you submit requests

reviews.text:
Like pretty much everything else in this town
A complete failure  when it comes to any enforcement 
Only reason why I report it is because a class action lawsuit will expose this city’s leadership complete disregard of their foremost duty to enforce our laws.
reviews.title: Good app no enforcement

reviews.text: I like the sight but I put the address of the property and it doesn’t display the right address
reviews.title: Addresses for the issues reported

reviews.text: After 6 attempts to create an account I finally deleted the app
reviews.title: Hjordunfuurvmhksrb

reviews.text:
The principal of the website is good however, site navigation is poor and the response to posted issues is non existent or extremely slow resolution. 
Some items have taken months or even years to to be addressed.
reviews.title: Slow response

reviews.text: A lawsuit is gonna come about - this app is worse than next door app allowing anonymous neighbor complaints that lead to unnecessary neighbors stabbing other neighbors in the back. Anything regarding that brings the attention of a code complaince Officer against another patron or neighbor should require information as the complainant- becuase the defendant might end up in court with charges and this constitutes a illegal action and retaliation against law abiding homeowners who are living their lives normally. Then a anonymous complaint only one causes a homeowner to be a criminal to code complaince and their City. Allows patrons to lie and “add brownies to the bend complaint to make it look more Thant what it is . For example : overgrown weeds Around a wrecked vehicle: when the vehicle isn’t wrecked or damaged. Allows white lies and over exaggerated descriptions. Causing bigger issues than necessary.

reviews.text: Needham DPW guys are the best & they take care of  of business

reviews.text: This is the only way my county will apparently let me report a problem, but it doesn’t work.  I downloaded the app and followed the instructions only to be told I already had an account.  How is that possible when I’ve never used the app before?  I then tried to register using my husband’s e-mail address & waited for a one time password to be sent.  It never happened.  I guess this is now a convenient way for our local government folks to hide behind their desks and get paid for doing nothing!
reviews.title: Doesn’t Work!

reviews.text: This used to be great but now I haven't been able to submit a report. The new "location validation" won't let me write the address and is making me try to choose an old report that was never closed that doesn't have the exact address. So when I hit submit I get an error message now saying the address isn't legitimate because it gives an address range and won't let me pinpoint a new location. (For illegal trash dumping). So many questions to ask and a waste of time now!
reviews.title: Used to work

reviews.text: I placed a request to have 4 shopping carts removed that were on Promenade Ave. The very next day, I received info that the task had been completed and resolved. The issue was resolved very quickly.
reviews.title: Quick response and action taken

reviews.text: I used the app to take a photo of a water leak to report and it even showed the photo in the preview. As soon as I hit submit it choose the last photo from my camera album and not the one I just took in the app, which was a photo for work with sensitive data in view! Now I can’t even delete the post and it is available for anyone to view! Emailed the support group and flagged the post but still no take down. I’m furious and no way should this have been allowed to happen
reviews.title: Uploaded a wrong photo!

reviews.text: The people in charge of this app could have also installed a delete button, to remove old requests over a year old. I keep getting emails on an outstanding request from 120+ days ago, just wanted it deleted. I reached out and they said they are not able to delete resolved requests. Why not?? Old resolved requests should be deleted. Please install a delete button and I’ll change my rating.
reviews.title: Good utility app but…

reviews.text: The police shows a great failure to get these type people off the street in a reasonable time frame!!

reviews.text: I am unable to even submit a report. It fails to submit and will not let me submit the answers to required questions. The one time I think I was able to get it to work (not sure how) it didn’t seem to help. City of Oakland didn’t get involved.

reviews.text: I like being able to help our local authorities address problem issues in the community. What I don’t like is  when these problems are disbursed to other authorities or departments and are immediately closed. To me, closing means completed. Leave the original call open so the public can follow the process from reporting to finished. This will also help those who are about to report the issue again know that the situation is already in process and they can follothe process too if they desire.
reviews.title: Closing Early

reviews.text: Thank you, as always, for Public  Works quick response

reviews.text: Easy to use and thankful it exists. Last thing I wanna do is sit on hold with the service provider to tend to an issue, or send an email and hope the person isn’t on vacation. The work gets done.

reviews.text: This application is generally good for reporting information to the city. However, I would suggest adding the ability to add videos. Also, the reporting party should be the one to clear the report because the city has been clearing reports as “resolved” when they receive the report, but prior to carrying out the work. We should be able to hold the city accountable by keeping track of the length of time it took from reporting the issue, to the city actually carrying out the work.
reviews.title: Missing some tools

Just a sad company and a sad city.

reviews.text: Completely useless.  It has my location as New Haven, CT (I’m in Atlanta, GA).  Won’t let me do anything to change it.  Can’t look up the request I submitted via web browser.  I’m sure New Haven is nice, Ive never been, but I honestly don’t care about their potholes.

reviews.text:
I’m still getting the same notices over and over for issues I posted 2+ years ago that we’re fixed even though I indicate that they have been fixed and should be CLOSED.
This has worked great for all the issues I’ve posted and should get 5 stars for that except for the continual notices. 
I’ll continue to use it when something comes up but once it’s fixed and I show it’s closed PLEASE stop sending me the same messages.
reviews.title: Used to Be Great

reviews.text:
Has stopped working with my local authorities and GPS locks are also taking longer or don’t work properly to get current location since last update.  The chance of all municipalities in two states no longer working with your system is pretty far fetched, so I’m going to chalk it up to an issue with the last update and location services.  You need to fix this!!!

App needs to be updated to use new iOS 12+ photo attachment API which no longer requires apps to have static and individualized permissions to our photo libraries.  The new API that’s been around since iOS 12 and is now in 13 as well, allows for the app to be given access to any photo the user chooses without needing permissions to the camera roll.  Your app doesn’t work properly when the new UI tries to serve it the photo the user chooses.  This needs to be fixed.

Worse app icon ever.  Am I fixing my heart or problems in the city/county I’m in? 🙄
reviews.title: Needs Update

reviews.text:
The previous reporting app used by Multnomah County was cumbersome and very difficult to navigate. Click/Fix is fantastic. Some of the best features are: 1)submitting a photo also provides the exact location
2)  logical screen sequence to report an issue
3) immediate submission acknowledgement and subsequent followup 
4) option to share posting with impacted neighbors

reviews.text:
While my hometown city doesn't use SeeClickFix for infrastructure deficiency reporting, I live in an area where 2 neighboring cities & 3rd city about an hour away uses SeeClickFix app platform. Instead of having multiple apps for multiple cities, government agencies, etc which use the SCF platform, this app does it all in one.
Update: Downgraded to 4 stars because the comments aren’t showing in the app.

reviews.text: I was able to photograph and locate an issue and submit the problem immediately to the city. On my morning walk the next day it had already been fixed. A wonderful useful and useable application!

reviews.text: After using this service for many years it seems more like a middle man to getting anything done, and many things seem to die with that middle man. If you have an issue in your town your better off heading down to the office to speak to a person the old fashioned way, or even giving a call would likely work better, heck sending a telegram might even work better! Overall I am not impressed by this App and find it has no need for existence.
reviews.title: Not Impressed

reviews.text:
The option to close a reported issue is not available in the iOS version of this app on a iPad Air 2. I had to login to the site using my computer and a browser to close incidents reporting potholes.

If that could be fixed or made more user friendly I would give it 5 stars.

reviews.text: It’s all or none with this tool, at least it gives us a place to vent and at times it works great, the problem is we are all overwhelmed, correct me if I’m wrong, but I see we’re making ground on illegal dumping I don’t see it like I used to, and I don’t hear or see post that dumping is still a major problem, still happens, but don’t see it as much.

reviews.text: This is nothing but a copied and renamed version of Commonwealth Connect. I would be OK with that if they at least fixed some of the glitches and shortcomings of Commonwealth Connect. First of all, there’s no intuitive way to sort through issues in your neighborhood. Second, there is really no help through the app to explain certain things. For example, what do the different colors of the pins actually represent? Also, why is it that when I receive an email notification I can’t just click on the link and automatically open the app to that issue? Instead, it opens a web browser which is much more difficult to navigate on a mobile device. I feel like this app is an excellent start to improving our community, but it really needs a better GUI.
reviews.title: Just renamed Commonwealth Connect

reviews.text: Noticed very large, very dead, tree about 15 yards from Rd that serves all school approaches to elementary school. Report brought removal within 2 weeks.

reviews.text:
After this app got a big upgrade recently, when you tapped a link in an email notification, it didn’t open the issue and there was no way to find it. Now it does open the issue, but when you tap to vote or change the status, nothing happens. 

Used to be useful, now it’s junk.
reviews.title: Broken

reviews.text: I give this app for stars for two reasons one There is no iPad app two They change my city app and deleted it without putting a notice on the app saying to download this one I have to find out on my own imagine people that are not tech savvy and would even think about to do that. But this app is better than the website still think it could be improved.
reviews.title: See click fix St. Pete app gone

reviews.text: App not very user friendly and is only effective as the agency’s using it!

reviews.text: This app probably drives the city guys crazy! However it is very efficient, useful, and appreciated by people like me!
reviews.title: Crazy!

reviews.text:
When I moved to Oakland I was surprised at the condition of the roads. I mentioned seeing some garbage left on the sidewalk and a neighbor told me about SeeClickFix!  

For a test I wrote up about a pothole. Within a week it was filled!  What a surprise!  I’ve been reporting all sorts of incidents like debris, potholes, and sidewalk tripping hazards.  

I’m impressed with the promptness of the response to fix. I also learned that if anything may be hazardous waste to contact the fire department- who came by the very next day to pick up the 5-gallon cans of used paint! 

The app is easy to use, similar to the website reporting.
reviews.title: Grrrreat to know this app!

reviews.text: Really love this app and the way I can keep track of reports I make to my city, I really feel like I’m helping improve my community. :)
reviews.title: Helps with transparency!

reviews.text: This app plus my smart phone camera unclogs storm sewers, gets a soggy mattress along the roadside picked up or reports blight. It works. Really does!
reviews.title: Click to Fix My Neighborhood

reviews.text: Even when I type in the address for service your app changes and puts the current location I’m at instead of the problem area. Very annoying!
reviews.title: Tagging spots on map

reviews.text: The app is good! That action is not! Nothing is done about what we submit.
reviews.title: Application

reviews.text:
Past:  great tool for reporting and getting minor issues resolved.
Present:  Poor tool where duplicate, ONGOING complaints are being erased and intentionally overlooked by the City ppl accessing this app.  They are finding reasons NOT to acknowledge and address your written, legitimate complaints. Trying to hide something... i don’t know what, but something. 

Post and post for help to no avail.  Several caring Complaints CLOSED with no resolution. This app is now a joke that is simply Not Funny. City taxes paid, but this City rudely stops you from posting a complaint until they review and revise what you are actually complaining about.  Would do better sending a News cam for exposure.  Trying to make Memphis a better place, but the real issues are covered up.  Unprofessionally awful in many ways!  You started this system- Why STOP now!
reviews.title: Good tool under scrutiny by City if Memphis staff responders

reviews.text: The map is shifted north south from reality in Memphis TN. So if you select north side of street it geocodes to south side of street.
reviews.title: Great but...

reviews.text: This used to be a great app! Now I can’t figure out how to report an issue to anyplace but my “neighbors.” I want to report issues to the city! My neighbors already know about the issues. The app is no longer intuitive AT ALL. I do not recommend it and will delete it from my phone as soon as I finish this review.
reviews.title: New version is incomprehensible

reviews.text: I was forced to use the app to report an issue to my city because SeeClickFix makes website reporting non-functional. I prefer to use web interfaces - mobile or laptop/desktop - to limit single-use app clutter on my phone, and to limit the data collection that apps generally are designed to maximize. SeeClickFix.com does have a web interface to report issues but the issue reporting function is hidden and not obvious. Instead the site devotes its real estate and resources to directing users to download the app. Once the website reporting page is found, it fails to function. I tried it on mobile Firefox, desktop Firefox and desktop Safari. Only after failing those three attempts did I download the app, because there is no alternative way to report a road construction problem in my city. I reported the issue and then deleted the app.  There is no need for this app - issues should be reportable through the website.  Some users may find it convenient but I see no reason why the issue reporting page could not be bookmarked in the browser of the user’s choice.
reviews.title: Unnecessary app

reviews.text: Wow!  Pickup within 24 hours of reporting on SeeClickFix!  Now that’s how it works!  Much appreciation!
reviews.title: Dumping on Cunningham at Davenport

reviews.text: The city just closes the reports and nothing has been done- this is a let down
reviews.title: Abandoned cars left for weeks

reviews.text: This is app is easy to use and allows you to put additional information to help explain the situation. Very helpful and creates a quick turn around time with the county while also providing feedback in a timely manner.
reviews.title: Great tool for the community

reviews.text:
No more calling since my daughter downloaded this app. 

Now I take a picture of the trash that others dump in my neighborhood and magically the fairy angels come and pick it up. Like magic, poof it’s gone, but there has been time that I had to resend. 

Thank you for this app City of Oakland.
reviews.title: Love this app

reviews.text:
It's easier to report on this app than on DC's 311 app, but on either one good luck getting better than a 10% success rate. I've reported around 150 items in the last 2 years in DC. Maybe 6 or 7 have been fixed. 

It's far too easy for cities to close out requests without doing anything at all. SeeClickFix should require a photo before cities can "close" a problem that has likely just been ignored.
reviews.title: Easy to report, hard to get anything fixed

reviews.text:
UPDATE: sadly I must revoke my review. For some reason the app was completely changed and ruined with a bizarre format, horrid layout and useless features. It was perfect before and now it's just useless. Who made this awful decision?

Easy to use, and literally the only thing that our local government has responded to. I love this app.
reviews.title: Really effective.

reviews.text: There needs to be a catch all  section for issues that don’t fit nicely into the selected issues. Dead fixed a water line break in front of my home last October/November and broke my curb and messed up my grass from the sidewalk to the street and I am unable to report this issue on the app. The work still has not been corrected and my front yard looks a mess. Please fix this issue
reviews.title: Need additional choices for issues to be reported

reviews.text: The city needs to respond in a more timely fashion. They ask for help but they also need to do their job. They keep asking for taxes but where are the results for us the citizens. Code enforcement forget about doing their job.
reviews.title: Please help us the Antioch residents!!!

reviews.text: No longer user friendly! Doesn’t accept addresses, reverts to reporters address. Picture sometimes will not load. Very difficult to use!!!!
reviews.title: ClickFix

reviews.text: I've been using this app for a few months. It is quite useful for getting those nasty potholes repaired. Like a lot of drivers I travel the same route every day. Always amazed that a road repair crew will just drive by the holes and not fix them. Worse, they'll fix one you report but leave another one five feet away and not fill it. This app makes it more efficient. Enable GPS and then Snap photos with your smartphone. Fill out the form and attach photos. GPS provides specific addresses (in most cases) so the crew can find it, compare to the photo(s) and get 'r done. I like it! Tires,?wheels, tie rods, springs. All that it very expensive to repair. Compared to the cost of fixing some holes this app provides a civic minded way to save yourself and thousands of other drivers costly repairs. Virginia Beach is very good about getting them fixed quickly, there is no reason not to use this app!
reviews.title: Great For Getting Potholes Repaired

reviews.text: App doesn't respond very quickly when tapping/pressing the icon to close reports.  One other person and myself are directing people to the appropriate place to report issues, because the city doesn't monitor SeeClickFix since they have their own app.

reviews.text: Thanks for this app. It makes life easier and my neighborhood better.

reviews.text: As much as I was hoping for real improvement, I think it’s actually gone backwards. It’s a disorganized app with even more disorganized search results. It’s all we’ve got, so I’ll have to keep using it, but it’s always frustrating.

reviews.text:
Updated app makes it harder to post issues 
The ability to check similar posts might be nice, but should be date sensitive

reviews.text: The app makes reporting easy but one problem I noticed was if i see something that needs to be reported and I’m driving at the time, i can snap a picture but i need to pull over in order to submit because the app will always take the GPS address even if i type in the correct address of the violation it won’t accept it.
reviews.title: Good app

reviews.text: I can not report anything that asked for an address as on my phone I can not edit the location. All I see when I go to edit it is the lengthy generic text the app has. I can not scroll to the bottoms to edit it. My only choice is to report using the wrong type of report saying everything is a road issue or a park issue even when it is with a property. I never had an issue in the past being able to enter the exact address. Hope this gets solved as I like the app and my city responds well to it.
reviews.title: Used to work well, now not so much

reviews.text: Cannot send any report. Continuously got a 500 server error.
reviews.title: Useless

reviews.text: The Town of Chelmsford “uses” the app, but nothing actually gets fixed. They are GREAT at replying that they looked at things though.

reviews.text: After the latest update, the GPS locator is off by about 50 ft after you click the submit button and check the report.

reviews.text:
It’s really dumb as the submitter of an issue that I cannot edit the title one submitted!

Other than that the app is a great idea, the biggest problem is that the local public works department‘s are not already linked in to this app.
reviews.title: Cannot edit title once submitted!

reviews.text: I really liked this app, but the latest update makes it much harder to use. I'll be looking for other solutions.
reviews.title: New update is a bust

reviews.text:
The new version is pretty, but did little to enhance the user experience. 

The messages and notifications feed is no longer. 
You can no longer view user profiles to see issues they submitted. 
You can no longer see top contributors in your community. 

Some more minor, but annoying features that disappeared:
Way to many taps and swipes to see an issue image. 
No way to zoom in on an issue image. 
No way to tap on user profiles/usernames in the comment thread. 
No way to see total number of issues I reported. 
And more 

As you can see, many features lost, but few gained other than a new look for the same tasks. While I provided this feedback in the beta stage, unfortunately it wasn't taken into account. I hope these features are on the road map and that this app see more updates than the last one. 

Keep up the good work.
reviews.title: New Version, Lost Features

reviews.text: This service gets a ZERO
reviews.title: Zero

reviews.text:
This app in theory could be good, however it is not. 

It no longer populates issues in areas, even after I have submitted one. 
The UI is poor. 
The design and layout is poor. 
There is no way to communicate with the person that closed or did something to the ticket.
reviews.title: Crap App

reviews.text:
The app is not perfect, but solid enough to get the job done…and it is so much better than previous systems I have used!

The app is only a tool that makes it much easier to report problems, so the very bad reviews based on how the cities actually respond (or don’t) to the reports are not at all fair to the creators of the app itself.
reviews.title: A good tool that cities may or may not use well

reviews.text: This app is incredibly useful in helping local government keep up with the times and serve its residents. Now if only my city paid more attention...
reviews.title: Love this app!

reviews.text: This apps seems useless.  I reported an illegal dump; big bundle of trash dumped in the middle of the road.  Several days later the app indicated that the issue was closed, but the trash was still there.  I called the office and they said that someone came out and pushed the garbage to the side to make the road safe.  WHAT?  I asked why did they not remove the trash.  He said maybe they didn't have the proper equipment.  WHAT?  WHAT did they think they were coming out for related to an illegal dump.  He said he would contact a supervisor and see if they can get this cleanup expedited.  Here it is a week later and still the trash remains.  I don't know if this problem is specific to Oakland, but I do know that it is very frustrating and suggestive of a dysfunctional app and city.
reviews.title: Is anybody home?

reviews.text:
Comments should be anonymous. You should be able to remove or edit your own comments. 

The officials can actually see your email if you report anonymous, defeating the anonymity.

There is no way in the app to change your email once your anonymity has been breached.
reviews.title: Needs improvement

reviews.text: I believe that the City needs to increase it fines and court cost to deter Violators.

reviews.text:
Addendum: city (Medford,  MA) does not respond to problems. Reported over a year ago by email and again this spring by SeeClickFix and sidewalk still a danger. Maybe it's not the app but the city admin. 

Cannot filter for your city or neighborhood very well except by maps. Hasn't had a new version since 2015. Disappointed.
reviews.title: Needs to be updated 👎🏼

reviews.text: I've reported streetlight outages, holes in sidewalks, and water leaks--all were taken care of by Oakland or Berkeley crews in short order. I'm still waiting for some pothole repairs...but that's not the app's fault.
reviews.title: Very effective!

reviews.text:
This app is great, as it allows townspeople to report issues in their neighbourhood.  A couple of things need improvement:

It would be a great improvement using the search function to be able to filter by nearest incidents, having the most recent reports at the top.  Currently they sort and filter by location.  One has to fish through the lot to find the newest.  If I filter by newest, i get reports from adjacent communities, and have to go fishing through to find those for my town.  There is no information on the list what town the incident relates to.

I realize this may be difficult, and may require some work.  Possibly set a radius (miles?) from current location? Or add some information to the list to know what town is reporting an issue.

The communities often do not seem to close fixed incidents.  I have an incident that was fixed but the town never closed the issue, even though it's been done for months. This is a town issue, and not an issue with the app.  Perhaps the user reporting can have the option to close the incident using the app.  That way more items might be closed.
reviews.title: Improvements

reviews.text: Easy to use, and my county government facilities office commented on my request within the hour.
reviews.title: Grateful

reviews.text: This app is great.   Written records get answered and the work completed.
reviews.title: Great app that allows constituent to report issues

reviews.text: App works great, easy to send issues and follow up on them. Chicago is not integrated well into this, though. Many issues are not listed and  issues get closed by the City when they haven't been resolved.
reviews.title: Great app, which the city did more

reviews.text: Love this app because I don't have to call when I need to correspond with the city regarding trash, pick up or new trash cans.
reviews.title: Can You Help Me?

reviews.text: Never had a problem. Easy to use. Provides a great service. I report potholes all the time. If you were no longer near the location, you can move the location of your report on the map. I would give it five stars, but I'm not 100% sure these reports or actually submitted to local governments or not.
reviews.title: Great, assuming reports are submitted

reviews.text:
Repeatedly receive "connection error" message when trying to add comment to open issue. 

Although you must create an account to use, there's no way to recall or review posts you've made, you have to find it from the map view. 

No way to limit the list of open tickets by issue.
reviews.title: Bugs, no way to recall issues you've posted

reviews.text:
When I attempted to report an issue, I filled out all the required fields.  There was no exact address - it was just in the middle of a road, no House number.  I listed the road name as the address.  It would not submit and kept popping up a field that said there was no location listed.  There was indeed a location listed, so I don't understand why it was giving me the error.  I had to use the Full Site (on a phone, which was a nightmare), and when I entered the exact same info, it submitted easily.
I cannot believe this is human error as I did complete all required fields and drop-downs in app reporting an Issue.  Please check into it.
reviews.title: Could not submit - bug in app?

reviews.text: This app seems to have been taken over by the school system here in Miami, Everything you post requires you to provide Name of School, Type of School, Are you the principal and your initials if you're the school administrator. This seems ridiculus to me and has been this way for well over a year. It has rendered this app useless in dade county in my opinion, perhaps maybe that was the intent.
reviews.title: School information

reviews.text: I like the convenience of reporting service issues. Keep up the good work!
reviews.title: Useful Reporting

reviews.text: You should have an updated profile section, and a search for people under the neighbors section. Don't know how long it takes for issues to be resolved.

reviews.text: I love the fact that there is a means for the public to report issues in Oakland that needs to be addressed. City employees can not be everywhere so having the public's eyes helps everyone who lives and works in Oakland. It has been my experience that the City responds in a timely manner whenever I've reported an issue.
reviews.title: Cleaner City

reviews.text: My SCF app fails consistently, most likely because they fail to keep up as OS's improve. I start a report and if there is a pic in it the app quits half way thru the report. The app has not been updated since 2014 according to this page.
